prompt:
[BRAND LOCK — identity invariant, composition open]
The provided reference image teaches the التوأم brand identity — it teaches WHAT the product is, not how this frame must look. Identity DNA, preserved across any camera angle: Two thick BROASTED golden-crispy chicken breast fillets stacked one above the other, each with a craggy deep-fried crumb crust, separated and crowned by melted cheese that drapes over the edges, bright-green sliced dill pickles peeking between the layers, and Albaik's twin sauces — the white garlic ثومية and the pinkish-orange cocktail (مسحب) sauce — oozing down the sides. The bun is Albaik's own pale, soft, SEEDLESS round bun (a plain glossy top crown, NOT a sesame burger bun), generously domed, against the brand's red backdrop. SIGNATURE CUE — the product must read by its white garlic sauce (ثومية).
Angle, framing, exact placement within the light pool, and the spotlight's precise character are creative decisions — choose what serves the theatrical reveal, keeping brand-readable surfaces oriented toward camera. INTEGRATION: the product is photographed inside this dark stage, not pasted onto it — the single spotlight that defines the void defines the product; its contact pool, falloff, and specular response all belong to the same light.

[REALISM & CAPTURE CHARACTER]
Capture character: Glossy high-gloss commercial fast-food photography: single hard key light producing crisp specular highlights on melted cheese, glistening sauce drips and sesame-bun sheen, deep contact shadows, and a saturated color-field backdrop with radial vignette. Surfaces look freshly fried and juicy (visible oil sheen, steam-implied moisture). Hero food shot close and slightly above eye level on a clean or reflective surface; ember-spark particles added on the spicy line; tightly retouched, appetite-maximized, not documentary/natural. — default clean_studio with theatrical lift. Material truth: crisp golden-brown crust with honest oil sheen and visible craggy texture; tender steam-moist interior where broken; true fried-food surface, never plastic-uniform; product-truth texture: broasted/crispy; signature white garlic sauce (ثومية) — the substrate behaves as its real self under the hard beam (film stays pliable with honest creases, glass refracts, carton edges stay crisp). The lit portion shows genuine specular response and real texture where the spotlight rakes it; the shadowed portion falls to honest black, not crushed digital void. No CGI-clean perfection, no plastic uniformity, no artificial glow halo.

[SCALE CALIBRATION]
Each burger ≈ 9–10 cm bun diameter, ≈ 6–7 cm tall (a single thin fillet, shorter than the crispy stack). Scale-anchor = it is always shown as a matched PAIR (two identical burgers), and beside the fries bag/can in combos; one burger ≈ 0.55× the ALBAIK can height. — the product reads at its true real-world size. The spotlight pool is scaled to the product: an intimate beam for a small item, a wider theatrical pool for a large one. The pool of light on the ground is proportionate to the object standing in it.

[FRAMING & PROXIMITY]
Hero through closeness: the product holds a commanding share of frame — the negative black space is dramatic, not empty distance. Frame close enough that the spotlit material texture reads. No dead space between viewer and subject; the darkness presses in around a near, present hero.

[COLOR & CONTRAST]
The product's brand color, drawn from Two brand-owned color fields only: (1) ALBAIK signature deep brick-crimson red (≈ a rich tomato-to-maroon red, NOT a generic KFC bright red — it reads slightly cooler and deeper), almost always rendered as a center-bright radial glow falling to dark maroon at the edges; (2) ALBAIK golden-yellow (the price-disc / wordmark / packaging-pattern yellow). A deep navy-blue field is the owned occasion variant (night/Eid/Ramadan/fish). White is the owned neutral (the packaging bag). A color-blocking chain may fill the frame ONLY from {crimson-red, golden-yellow, navy-blue, white} — never a borrowed reference red/orange., is the only chromatic event in the entire frame — full saturation against total black; every other pixel is darkness. No borrowed accent colors, no secondary chromatic elements. Maximum drama through chromatic isolation.

[LIGHT & LENS]
ONE hard overhead spotlight, slightly forward, warm-white theatrical character with tight beam and rapid falloff — no fill; shadows fall to true black. A soft pool grounds the product beneath. Lens choice serves intimacy and compression — the model chooses focal feel and depth treatment that keeps the product crisp and the void absolute.

[NATURAL PLACEMENT]
The product stands or rests as gravity and its own form dictate within the pool — a sachet may lean or lie with honest folds, a bottle stands plumb, a jar sits square. No forced floating, no impossible balance.

[SCENE]
Creative seed (from the system): عزومة عائلية
Theatrical single-spotlight composition. Product within a tight pool of hard light on an invisible dark surface, the light falling off rapidly into pure surrounding black. Strong negative space of pure black. The mood is reverent, theatrical, main-stage. Choice points for the model: beam angle nuance, product orientation within the pool, pool size and edge softness.

[SUBJECT]
Single product as the sole illuminated subject. Material rendered with full physical truth where the light touches; shadowed portions fall naturally dark while form stays readable.

[BRAND CONSTRAINTS]
Single product only. No human elements, no props, no secondary objects. Background pure black void. The single spotlight is the only light. no cartoon/illustration/stylized rendering, no oversaturation, no plastic CGI uniformity, no borrowed reference colours, no readable text on neighbouring objects

[OUTPUT]
Photoreal theatrical product photography, maximum fidelity. Deep-black cinema grade with the brand color punching at full saturation. No overlay text, no watermark, no logo other than the product's own.

[SAUDI ADAPTATION — conditional]
authentic contemporary Saudi setting — true materials, never generic-Arabian — minimal environmental localization. For oud/fragrance/heritage sectors the spotlight may warm toward candlelight reverence. warmer gold/sand/amber tones can warm the beam toward gold.

[CREATIVE VARIANCE DIRECTIVE]
Across multiple generations of this chain, vary your choices: beam angle, product orientation, pool tightness, camera height, distance. Identity stays locked; the theatrical reveal should never look the same twice.

blocks: BRAND LOCK — identity invariant, composition open
